title: Common String Functions
description: Measure, search, extract, and replace parts of strings.

slides.title: Essential String Functions
slides.content:
# strlen() – String Length

`strlen(string $string): int` returns the length of a string in bytes (not characters). For multibyte encodings like UTF-8, use `mb_strlen()`.

```php
<?php
echo strlen("Hello"); // 5
?>
```

# strpos() – Find Position

`strpos(string $haystack, string $needle, int $offset = 0): int|false` returns the numeric position of the first occurrence of `$needle` in `$haystack`. Returns `false` if not found.

```php
<?php
$pos = strpos("Hello world", "world"); // 6 (0-based)
if ($pos !== false) { ... }
?>
```

slides.title: Using strlen and strpos
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$str = "Hello, PHP learners!";
echo strlen($str);
$pos = strpos($str, "PHP");
if ($pos !== false) {
    echo "Found at position $pos";
}
?>

slides.title: substr() – Extract Part
slides.content:
# substr() – Substring

`substr(string $string, int $offset, ?int $length = null): string` returns part of a string.

*   `$offset`: start position (0-based). Negative offset counts from the end.
*   `$length`: number of characters to return. If omitted, returns to the end.

```php
<?php
echo substr("abcdef", 1, 3);    // "bcd"
echo substr("abcdef", -2);      // "ef"
?>
```

slides.title: substr Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$email = "user@example.com";
$username = substr($email, 0, strpos($email, "@"));
echo $username;
$domain = substr($email, strpos($email, "@") + 1);
echo $domain;
?>

slides.title: str_replace() – Search and Replace
slides.content:
# str_replace()

`str_replace(mixed $search, mixed $replace, mixed $subject, int &$count = null): mixed` replaces all occurrences of `$search` with `$replace` in `$subject`.

It can work on strings or arrays.

```php
<?php
echo str_replace("world", "PHP", "Hello world"); // "Hello PHP"

$count = 0;
$new = str_replace("l", "L", "hello", $count);
echo $new; // "heLLo"
echo $count; // 2
?>
```

quizData.question: What will `strpos("Hello", "H")` return?
quizData.explanation: strpos is 0-based, so the first character is at position 0.

quizData.question: What does `substr("abcdef", -2, 1)` return?
quizData.explanation: Start at position -2 (second from last, which is 'e') and take 1 character, so 'e'.
